title: Workdoc - Progress Column Value not showing properly
excerpt: Hi all - I built a workdoc template that’s pulling multiple column values in different places of the doc. However, the Progress column value isn’t showing correctly. For instance, the progress bar for this item is at 100…

posts.author: simone.steffen (Simone Steffen)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi all - I built a workdoc template that’s pulling multiple column values in different places of the doc. However, the Progress column value isn’t showing correctly. For instance, the progress bar for this item is at 100%; but in the doc, it’s showing “No value”. I’ve left the “Date Submitted” in the screenshot to show that other column values are being pulled correctly.

Has anyone faced a similar issue?

image533×106 5.53 KB

image558×148 9.68 KB

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml:
This is a great question. I’m just now starting to use Docs so I don’t have a ton of experience. I was able to replicate your concern, so it seems to be something that hasn’t been worked out (maybe put in a feature request for that)? I even tried to see if I could get an AI or formula column to pull the progress % and use that instead but neither of those worked.

I’m going to share this with my Ambassador group and see if anyone has any ideas.

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml:
hi @simone.steffen

The progress bar column is 100% frontend (created in your browser) and can’t be read from any place (backend, API, docs, etc)

posts.author: simone.steffen (Simone Steffen)
posts.cookedHtml:
@basdebruin explained it:

The progress bar column is 100% frontend (created in your browser) and can’t be read from any place (backend, API, docs, etc)

While that explains the error, my Product Management mind immediately goes to “if that’s the case, it shouldn’t be listed as a valid field to be added as a column value”. It either works, or it shouldn’t be an option for users
